title: Ion Thruster TVAC Hot-Fire
summary: Integrated hot-fire campaign for an ion thruster, xenon feed system, power processing unit, and chamber support equipment.

steps.title: Confirm test authorization
steps.description: Verify the approved procedure revision, configuration baseline, test roles, communication loop, operating limits, abort criteria, emergency response, and required permits.

steps.title: Verify chamber readiness
steps.description: Confirm chamber cleanliness, pumping train status, cryogenic shroud configuration, plume target or beam dump, protective liners, viewports, and contamination controls.

image.alt: Interior of NASA Glenn Vacuum Chamber 5 with cryogenic panels and pumping equipment.
image.caption: Reference chamber view showing cryogenic surfaces and facility hardware used to sustain an electric-propulsion vacuum environment.

steps.title: Inspect thruster installation
steps.description: Verify thruster mounting, alignment, fastener witness marks, grounding and bonding, thermal interfaces, harness routing, and plume-clearance envelope.

steps.title: Verify xenon feed system
steps.description: Confirm xenon grade and source identification, regulator and valve configuration, leak-check acceptance, calibrated flow-control hardware, purge status, and pressure-relief provisions.

steps.title: Verify PPU and high voltage
steps.description: Inspect power processing unit harnessing, connector mating, polarity, bonding, insulation, current limiting, high-voltage interlocks, and emergency shutdown chain.

steps.title: Validate instrumentation
steps.description: Verify calibration status, channel mapping, sample rates, range settings, and common time reference for pressure, xenon flow, voltage, current, temperature, and facility telemetry.

steps.title: Complete chamber closeout
steps.description: Perform FOD and tool accountability, photograph the as-installed configuration, confirm personnel clear, verify access closures, and establish the chamber exclusion zone.

steps.title: Establish vacuum condition
steps.description: Pump down using the authorized sequence. Monitor outgassing, pressure trends, electrical isolation, and thermal conditions until the approved ignition environment is stable.

steps.title: Perform low-voltage checkout
steps.description: With beam extraction inhibited, verify command and telemetry paths, valve actuation, heater functions, housekeeping telemetry, and cathode or neutralizer conditioning as authorized.

steps.title: Authorize ignition
steps.description: When authorized, initiate xenon flow and discharge ignition, confirm stable cathode and neutralizer operation, then enable beam extraction at the prescribed low-power point.

image.alt: Xenon ion thruster operating in a vacuum chamber with a visible blue plume.
image.caption: Reference view of a xenon ion engine operating under vacuum; use it to orient the operator to the expected illuminated discharge and plume region.

steps.title: Execute throttle sequence
steps.description: Run the approved throttle points and dwell periods. Record discharge, beam, accelerator, neutralizer, xenon-flow, pressure, and thermal data; monitor for oscillation, arcing, or limit excursions.

steps.title: Verify restart transients
steps.description: Perform only the approved hot or cold restart cases. Capture ignition delay, overshoot, settling behavior, valve response, and any protection-system activation.

steps.title: Execute controlled shutdown
steps.description: Follow the authorized power-down order, confirm beam and discharge removal, verify stored high-voltage energy is discharged, apply xenon post-flow if required, and secure the feed system.

steps.title: Inspect and close out
steps.description: After approved chamber venting, inspect grids, neutralizer, insulators, harnesses, deposits, and evidence of arcing or erosion. Reconcile data files, photographs, anomalies, and final configuration.
